{{Short description|Hungarian linguist}}

'''Kata Csizér''' (17. November 1971)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mta.hu/koztestuleti_tagok?PersonId=10054286|title=MTA - Csizér Kata|date=27 January 2022|publisher=mta.hu|access-date=28 January 2022|archive-date=7 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220107025319/https://mta.hu/koztestuleti_tagok?PersonId=10054286|url-status=live}}</ref> is a Hungarian [[linguist]].<ref>{{cite web |url= https://doktori.hu/index.php?menuid=192&lang=EN&sz_ID=2822 |title= Kata Csizér |date= 17 December 2020 |publisher= Országos Doktori Tanács |access-date= 1 July 2020 |archive-date= 2 July 2020 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20200702171249/https://doktori.hu/index.php?menuid=192&lang=EN&sz_ID=2822 |url-status= live }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= https://www.waterstones.com/author/kata-csizer/770512 |title= Kata Csizer books and biography |date= 27 December 2020 |publisher= waterstones.com |access-date= 27 December 2020 |archive-date= 24 June 2021 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20210624195334/https://www.waterstones.com/author/kata-csizer/770512 |url-status= live }}</ref> She is currently a professor at the [[ELTE School of English and American Studies|School of English and American Studies]] of the [[ELTE Faculty of Humanities|Faculty of Humanities]] of the [[Eötvös Loránd University]], Hungary.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.btk.elte.hu/munkatarsak/wein-katalin-dr|title=Csizér - Eötvös Loránd University|date=24 June 2020|publisher=elte.hu|access-date=1 July 2020|archive-date=2 July 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200702032546/https://www.btk.elte.hu/munkatarsak/wein-katalin-dr|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://deal.elte.hu/pages/faculty_staff.html#CsK|title=Csizér - Eötvös Loránd University|date=24 June 2020|publisher=elte.hu|access-date=1 July 2020|archive-date=2 July 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200702173450/http://deal.elte.hu/pages/faculty_staff.html#CsK|url-status=live}}</ref> Her research focuses on [[applied linguistics]] with a special focus on [[motivation in second-language learning]] and teaching students with special needs.

Csizér is considered an authority along with [[Zoltán Dörnyei]] because of her work on [[motivation in second-language learning]]. She is the author along with [[Zoltán Dörnyei]] and Nóra Németh, of three seminal articles (reaching more than 1,000 citations in the field of second language learning) on motivation in second language learning. Apart from motivation in second language learning, she is also noted for her work on the inclusion of deaf learners into the foreign language classroom.<ref>{{cite journal|title= Foreign Language Learning Characteristics of Deaf and Severely Hard-of-Hearing Students |journal=The Modern Language Journal|volume=104|pages=233–249|publisher=Wiley Online Library|language=English|date=11 February 2020|doi= 10.1111/modl.12630 |last1=Csizér|first1=Kata|last2=Kontra H.|first2=Edit|doi-access=free|hdl=10831/47380|hdl-access=free}}</ref>

== Career ==
In 2000, one of her first publications was on history in which she investigated the change of the regime on the three [[Visegrád Group]] countries namely, [[Poland]], [[Czech Republic]], [[Slovakia]], and [[Hungary]].<ref>https://www.tarki.hu/adatbank-h/kutjel/pdf/a859.pdf {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624195914/https://www.tarki.hu/adatbank-h/kutjel/pdf/a859.pdf |date=2021-06-24 }} {{Bare URL PDF|date=March 2022}}</ref>

In 2009 her work on motivation in second language learning was published on the website of the Institute of Research on Education (in Hungarian: Oktatáskutató és Fejlesztő Intézet).<ref>{{cite web |url= https://ofi.oh.gov.hu/tudastar/csizer-kata |title= A nyelvtanulási motiváció viszgálata |date= 17 June 2009 |publisher= Oktatáskutató és fejlesztő Intézet |access-date= 17 December 2020 |archive-date= 24 June 2021 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20210624201404/https://ofi.oh.gov.hu/tudastar/csizer-kata |url-status= live }}</ref>

Csizér obtained her [[PhD degree]] at the Eötvös Loránd University in 2004 and has been employed at the Department of English Applied Linguistics of the School of English and American Studies at the Eötvös Loránd University in [[Budapest]] since.<ref name="doktori"/>

In 2012 she obtained a Habilitation degree at the Eötvös Loránd University.<ref name="doktori">{{cite web|title=Kata Csizér – Eötvös Loránd University University|url=https://doktori.hu/index.php?menuid=192&lang=EN&sz_ID=2822|publisher=doktori.hu|language=Hungarian|date=23 June 2020|access-date=24 June 2020|archive-date=2 July 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200702171249/https://doktori.hu/index.php?menuid=192&lang=EN&sz_ID=2822|url-status=live}}</ref>

Since 2011, she has been an active member of the [[International Association of Teachers of English as a Foreign Language]] (IATEFL) Hungary, presenting at the organisation's conferences annually.<ref>{{cite web|title=IATEFL Hungary - Repertory|url=https://www.iatefl.hu/sites/default/files/Repertory_IATEFLHungary2015.pdf|publisher=iatefl.hu|language=English|date=23 June 2015|access-date=24 December 2020|archive-date=28 October 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211028030757/https://www.iatefl.hu/sites/default/files/Repertory_IATEFLHungary2015.pdf|url-status=live}}</ref>

On 29 August 2014, she was a plenary speaker along with [[Diane Larsen-Freeman]], [[Marjolijn Verspoor]], [[Kees de Bot]], [[Judit Kormos]] and [[Rosa Manchón]] at the International Conference on Motivational Dynamics  and Second Language Acquisition, organized by [[Zoltán Dörnyei]] at the [[University of Nottingham]].<ref>{{cite web|title= International Conference on Motivational Dynamics and Second Language Acquisition|url= https://www.nottingham.ac.uk/conference/fac-arts/english/international-conference-on-motivational-dynamics-and-second-language-acquisition/documents/conference-programme---18-august-2014.pdf|date= 18 August 2014|publisher= University of Nottingham|access-date= 27 December 2020|archive-date= 21 January 2022|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20220121133234/https://www.nottingham.ac.uk/conference/fac-arts/english/international-conference-on-motivational-dynamics-and-second-language-acquisition/documents/conference-programme---18-august-2014.pdf|url-status= live}}</ref>

Csizér has been the associate editor of the [[Journal of Second Language Writing]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Journal of Second Language Writing - Editorial Board|url=https://www.journals.elsevier.com/journal-of-second-language-writing/editorial-board|publisher=Elsevier|language=English|date=23 June 2020|access-date=18 September 2020|archive-date=11 July 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220711152554/https://www.journals.elsevier.com/journal-of-second-language-writing/editorial-board|url-status=live}}</ref>

In 2018, she was invited keynote speaker at Faculty of Arty and Social Sciences of the [[National University of Singapore]] along with Phil Benson, Kimberly Noels and Xiaohong Wen.<ref>http://www.fas.nus.edu.sg/cls/CLaSIC/clasic2018/keynote_speakers2018.pdf {{Bare URL PDF|date=March 2022}}</ref>

On 28 May 2019, she was a guest lecturer at the [[University of Graz]], invited by Sarah Mercer.<ref>{{cite web|title= The Guest Lecture by Dr. Kata Csizér was a great success|url= https://anglistik.uni-graz.at/en/elt-research-and-methodology/news/detail/article/the-guest-lecture-by-dr-kata-csizer-was-a-great-success/|date= 28 May 2019|publisher= University of Graz}}{{Dead link|date=March 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

In 2019, she was a keynote speaker at the [[British Association for Applied Linguistics]] Special Interest Group conference at the [[University of Bath]], [[Bath, Somerset]], [[United Kingdom]]. She presented on the English language teachers’ motivation in Hungary: The results of a mixed-methods study.<ref>{{cite web|title=Keynote sessions for the BAAL Special Interest Group conference|url=https://www.bath.ac.uk/corporate-information/keynote-sessions-for-the-baal-special-interest-group-conference/|publisher=University of Bath|language=English|date=18 June 2019|access-date=27 December 2020|archive-date=12 August 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200812042418/https://www.bath.ac.uk/corporate-information/keynote-sessions-for-the-baal-special-interest-group-conference/|url-status=live}}</ref>

On 4 June 2019, she was a plenary speaker at the DTU CALIDIE Lectures in Multilingualism and Learning at the [[University of Luxembourg]].<ref>{{cite web|title=DTU CALIDIE Lectures in Multilingualism and Learning|url=https://wwwen.uni.lu/fhse/news_events/dtu_calidie_lectures_in_multilingualism_and_learning_the_motivational_profiles_of_hungarian_secondary_school_students_the_results_of_a_pilot_study_investigating_the_role_of_language_learning_dispositions_and_emotions|publisher=University of Luxembourg|language=English|date=23 June 2019|access-date=24 June 2020|archive-date=22 June 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200622082408/https://wwwen.uni.lu/fhse/news_events/dtu_calidie_lectures_in_multilingualism_and_learning_the_motivational_profiles_of_hungarian_secondary_school_students_the_results_of_a_pilot_study_investigating_the_role_of_language_learning_dispositions_and_emotions|url-status=live}}</ref>

In 2021, she was an invited speaker at the METU ELT Convention at the [[Middle East Technical University]] in [[Ankara]], [[Turkey]].<ref>{{cite web|title=14th METU ELT Convention|url=https://dbe.metu.edu.tr/convention2020/plenary-speakers.html|publisher=metu.edu.tr|language=English|date=23 June 2020|access-date=24 June 2020|archive-date=12 June 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200612193907/http://www.dbe.metu.edu.tr/convention2020/plenary-speakers.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

In 2021, she was a plenary speaker, along with [[Tammy Gregersen]], Phil Hiver, and [[Sue Roffey]] at the Language Teacher Psychology conference at the [[University of Graz]], [[Graz]], [[Austria]].<ref>{{ cite web|title = University of Graz - Plenary and invited speakers |url= https://language-teacher-psychology-2021.uni-graz.at/de/neuigkeiten/detail/article/plenary-and-invited-speakers/|date= 28 May 2020 |publisher= University of Graz}}</ref>

== Research ==
===Motivation===
Csizér wrote three articles which are considered seminal articles (more than 1000 citations in the field of second language learning) on [[Motivation in second-language learning|motivation in second language learning]]. One of them is, co-authored with [[Zoltán Dörnyei]], entitled ''Ten commandments for motivating language learners: results of an empirical study'', published in [[Language Teaching Research]] in 1998. In this study, the authors conducted a survey to obtain classroom data on motivational strategies. 200 Hungarian teachers of English completed the questionnaire on how important they consider a selection of 51 strategies and how often they use them in their classes. Based on the survey, the authors compiled a brief set of ten motivational macrostrategies, which they called 'Ten commandments for motivating language learners'.<ref>{{cite journal|title= Ten commandments for motivating language learners: results of an empirical study |journal=Language Teaching Research|volume=2|pages=203–229|publisher=SAGE journals|language=English|date=1 July 1998|doi=10.1177/136216889800200303|last1=Dörnyei|first1=Zoltán|last2=Csizér|first2=Kata|issue=3|s2cid=9835663}}</ref>

The second most cited work by Csizér is entitled ''The Internal Structure of Language Learning Motivation and Its Relationship with Language Choice and Learning Effort'', published in [[The Modern Language Journal]]. The co-author of this article, published in 2005, is Zoltán Dörnyei. The authors argued that language learning motivation is a complex construct. They evaluated a proposed theoretical model concerning the internal structure of the second language motivation complex and its effects on motivated behaviour by using structural equation modelling. The main results of their study was that integrativeness might be the single most important factor, subsuming the effects of all the other responses to questions asked.<ref>{{cite journal|title= The Internal Structure of Language Learning Motivation and Its Relationship with Language Choice and Learning Effort |journal=The Modern Language Journal|volume=89|pages=19–36|publisher=Wiley Online Library|language=English|date=16 February 2005|doi= 10.1111/j.0026-7902.2005.00263.x |last1=Csizér|first1=Kata|last2=Dörnyei|first2=Zoltán}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/3588549|title=The Internal Structure of Language Learning Motivation and Its Relationship with Language Choice and Learning Effort|date=2005|publisher=jstor.org|jstor=3588549|last1=Csizér|first1=Kata|last2=Dörnyei|first2=Zoltán|journal=The Modern Language Journal|volume=89|issue=1|pages=19–36|doi=10.1111/j.0026-7902.2005.00263.x|url-access=subscription|access-date=2020-12-26|archive-date=2022-04-03|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220403082825/https://www.jstor.org/stable/3588549|url-status=live}}</ref>

Csizér's third most cited work is entitled ''Motivation, language attitudes and globalisation'', published by Multilingual Matters in 2006. The co-authors of this book was Zoltán Dörnyei and Nóra Németh. In this volume the results of the largest ever [[Language attitudes|language attitude]] and motivation study was presented, involving over 13,000 adolescent language learners in Hungary. The participants were surveyed in 1993, 1999 and 2004. The authors claimed that the results are not confined to the European context but have much wider implications regarding attitude change, motivational dynamics and language globalisation.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Dörnyei|first1=Zoltán|last2=Csizér|first2=Kata|last3=Németh|first3=Nóra|title=Motivation, Language Attitudes and Globalisation: A Hungarian Perspective|year=2006|publisher=Multilingual Matters|isbn=9781853598852|pages=224|url=https://www.multilingual-matters.com/page/detail/Motivation-Language-Attitudes-and-Globalisation/?k=9781853598852|access-date=2020-12-25|archive-date=2021-01-17|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210117213629/https://www.multilingual-matters.com/page/detail/Motivation-Language-Attitudes-and-Globalisation/?k=9781853598852|url-status=live}}</ref>

In an article published in The Modern Language Journal, Csizér argued along with the co-authors [[Judit Kormos]] and Ágnes Sarkadi, that goals, attitudes, and motivation might be seen as a closely interconnected co-adaptive system, in which change in one of these motivational constructs also trigger change in the other closely related constructs. Their article was one of the first manuscripts which attempted to analyze motivation from a [[Complex Dynamic Systems Theory]] perspective.<ref>{{cite journal|title= The Dynamics of Language Learning Attitudes and Motivation: Lessons From an Interview Study of Dyslexic Language Learners |journal=The Modern Language Journal|volume=94|pages=470–487|publisher=Wiley Online Library|language=English|date=16 October 2010|doi= 10.1111/j.1540-4781.2010.01054.x |last1=Csizér|first1=Kata|last2=Kormos|first2=Judit|last3=Sarkadi|first3=Ágnes|issue=3}}</ref>

Csizér claimed, along with Judit Kormos in article published in [[TESOL Quarterly]] in 2014, that strong instrumental goals and international posture, along with positive future self‐guides, might be the prerequisites for use of effective [[Self-regulated learning|self‐regulatory strategies]], which in turn might have an important role in influencing autonomous use of traditional and computer‐assisted learning resources.<ref>{{cite journal|title= The Interaction of Motivation, Self-Regulatory Strategies, and Autonomous Learning Behavior in Different Learner Groups |journal=TESOL Quarterly|volume=48|pages=275–299|publisher=Wiley Online Library|language=English|date=June 2014|doi= 10.1002/tesq.129 |last1=Kormos|first1=Judit|last2=Csizér|first2=Kata|issue=2|doi-access=free|hdl=10831/66125|hdl-access=free}}</ref>

===Deaf learners===
Another research area in which Csizér advanced the field of applied linguistics is the language learning of deaf learners. Along with Kontráné Hegybíró and Piniel, they wrote the first book on the deaf learners' language learning.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Kontráné Hegybíró|first1=Edit|last2=Csizér|first2=Kata|last3=Piniel|first3=Kata|title=Hallássérült fiatalok idegennyelv tanulása Magyarországon|year=2015|publisher=Tinta|isbn=9789634090212|pages=150|url=https://moly.hu/konyvek/kontrane-hegybiro-edit-csizer-kata-piniel-katalin-szerk-hallasserult-fiatalok-idegennyelv-tanulasa-magyarorszagon|access-date=2020-09-29|archive-date=2020-10-24|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201024230542/https://moly.hu/konyvek/kontrane-hegybiro-edit-csizer-kata-piniel-katalin-szerk-hallasserult-fiatalok-idegennyelv-tanulasa-magyarorszagon|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal|title= Hallássérült nyelvtanulók egyéni változóinak vizsgálata: motiváció, képzetek és stratégiák |journal=Alkalmazott Nyelvtudomány|volume=13|pages=5–21|publisher=Tanulmányok|language=English|date=2013|doi=  |last1=Kontráné Hegybíró|first1=Edit|last2=Csizér|first2=Kata|last3=Piniel|first3=Kata}}</ref>

== Publications ==
=== Books ===
*Dörnyei, Z., Csizér, K., & Németh, N. (2006). ''Motivation, language attitudes and globalisation: A Hungarian perspective''. Multilingual Matters.
*Csizér, K., & Magid, M. (2014). ''The Impact of Self-Concept on Language Learning''. Channel View Publications.
*Lamb, M., Csizér, K., Henry, A., & Ryan, S. (2019). ''The Palgrave handbook of motivation for language learning''. Palgrave Macmillan.
*Csizér, K. (2020). ''Second language learning motivation in a European context: The case of Hungary''. Springer.

=== Articles ===
*Dörnyei, Z., & Csizér, K. (1998). Ten commandments for motivating language learners: results of an empirical study. ''Language Teaching Research'', ''2''(3), 203–229. [https://doi.org/10.1177/136216889800200303 Ten commandments for motivating language learners: results of an empirical study]
*Dörnyei, Z., & Csizér, K. (2002). Some Dynamics of Language Attitudes and Motivation: Results of a Longitudinal Nationwide Survey, ''Applied Linguistics'', ''23''(4), 421–462, [https://doi.org/10.1093/applin/23.4.421 Some Dynamics of Language Attitudes and Motivation: Results of a Longitudinal Nationwide Survey]
*Csizér, K., & Dörnyei, Z. (2005). Language Learners’ Motivational Profiles and Their Motivated Learning Behavior. ''Language Learning'', ''55''(4), 613–659. [https://doi.org/10.1111/j.0023-8333.2005.00319.x Language Learners’ Motivational Profiles and Their Motivated Learning Behavior]
*Csizér, K., & Dörnyei, Z. (2005). The Internal Structure of Language Learning Motivation and Its Relationship with Language Choice and Learning Effort. ''The Modern Language Journal'', 89(1), 19–36. [https://doi.org/10.1111/j.0026-7902.2005.00263.x The Internal Structure of Language Learning Motivation and Its Relationship with Language Choice and Learning Effort]
*Kormos, J., & Csizér, K. (2006). An interview study of inter-cultural contact and its role in language learning in a foreign language environment. System, 35(2), 241–258. [https://doi.org/10.1016/j.system.2006.10.010 An interview study of inter-cultural contact and its role in language learning in a foreign language environment]
*Kormos, J., & Csizér, K. (2008). Age‐Related Differences in the Motivation of Learning English as a Foreign Language: Attitudes, Selves, and Motivated Learning Behavior. Language Learning, 58(2), 327–355. [https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1467-9922.2008.00443.x Age-Related Differences in the Motivation of Learning English as a Foreign Language: Attitudes, Selves, and Motivated Learning Behavior]
*Csizér, K., & Kormos, J. (2008). Modelling the Role of Inter-Cultural Contact in the Motivation of Learning English as a Foreign Language. Applied Linguistics, 30(2), 166–185. [https://doi.org/10.1093/applin/amn025 Modelling the Role of Inter-Cultural Contact in the Motivation of Learning English as a Foreign Language]
*Csizér, K., & Lukács, G. (2009). The comparative analysis of motivation, attitudes and selves: The case of English and German in Hungary. System, 38(1), 1–13. [https://doi.org/10.1016/j.system.2009.12.001 The comparative analysis of motivation, attitudes and selves: The case of English and German in Hungary]
*Csizér, K., & Kormos, J., & Sarkadi, Á. (2010). The Dynamics of Language Learning Attitudes and Motivation: Lessons From an Interview Study of Dyslexic Language Learners. The Modern Language Journal, 94(3), 470–487. [https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1540-4781.2010.01054.x The Dynamics of Language Learning Attitudes and Motivation: Lessons From an Interview Study of Dyslexic Language Learners]
*Kormos, J., Kiddle, T., & Csizér, K. (2011). Systems of Goals, Attitudes, and Self-related Beliefs in Second-Language-Learning Motivation. Applied Linguistics, 32(5), 495–516  [https://doi.org/10.1093/applin/amr019 Systems of Goals, Attitudes, and Self-related Beliefs in Second-Language-Learning Motivation]
*Kormos, J., & Csizér, K. (2013). The interaction of motivation, self‐regulatory strategies, and autonomous learning behavior in different learner groups. TESOL Quarterly, 48(2). 275–299.
*You, C. J., Dörnyei, Z., & Csizér, K. (2015). Motivation, Vision, and Gender: A Survey of Learners of English in China. ''Language Learning'', ''66''(1), 94–123. [https://doi.org/10.1111/lang.12140 Motivation, Vision, and Gender: A Survey of Learners of English in China]
